data.title: Jimmy Kimmel's show is paused with 315 staff furloughed due to budget cuts. Show's future uncertain.
data.description: In the latest update, it has been reported that there has been a significant increase in the number of COVID-19 cases in the city. Health officials have confirmed that there are now 500 new cases, bringing the total number of cases in the city to 10,000. This surge in cases has prompted the government to implement stricter measures to curb the spread of the virus. Additionally, there have been 20 new deaths reported, bringing the total number of fatalities to 250. Authorities are urging the public to adhere to safety guidelines and take necessary precautions to protect themselves and others from the virus.

data.title: Hamburger Helper sales surge as Americans cut costs amid rising meat prices and turn to canned meats and retro favorites.
data.description: Consumers are facing rising costs of beef and other grocery items as prices continue to climb. In response, many are opting for more affordable alternatives such as canned meats and a classic staple from the 1970s. This shift in consumer behavior comes as the cost of beef has increased by X% in recent months, leading many to seek cost-effective options for their grocery needs.

data.title: Plan smartly for splurges in uncertain economy. Avoiding expensive vacations can help save, considering the tough times.
data.description: During uncertain economic times, spending lavishly on costly trips or gourmet meals might seem imprudent. However, with careful consideration and budgeting, individuals can still enjoy indulgent experiences without breaking the bank.

data.title: Oil sanctions have increased Russia's shadow fleet, with costs rising for shipping by sea. Illicit economy grows.
data.description: New regulations have increased the expenses for Russia to transport oil through the sea. As a result, there has been a growth in illegal shipping activities, leading to significant consequences.

data.description: On September 20, 2025, in Cleveland, a test message was released by PR Newswire multiple times. The repetition of the message indicated a test communication being conducted for evaluation purposes. The information conveyed did not pertain to any specific news content but was a repetition of a test message. data.title: Billionaire Ray Dalio warns U.S. about $37.5 trillion debt crisis risk, expects no spending cuts despite concerns.
data.description: During the Future China Global Forum in Singapore, Ray Dalio, the founder of Bridgewater Associates, raised concerns about the U.S.'s massive $37.5 trillion debt, warning that it could lead to a potential crisis. The billionaire hedge fund manager has previously cautioned about the risk of a "debt-induced heart attack" in the near future. Despite increasing worries about fiscal sustainability, Dalio doesn't believe the U.S. will make significant spending cuts. He highlighted that due to various reasons, the U.S. cannot reduce its spending, which is setting a dangerous fiscal path. Notably, the 76-year-old billionaire attributed the debt issue to "human nature" rather than failures in policy from different political parties. For more details, refer to the full story on Benzinga.com.
